*** 2 INJ *** SIX MOBILE HOMES WERE SHIFTED OFF OF THEIR FOUNDATIONS WITH SOME RECEIVING EXTENSIVE ROOF DAMAGE. THERE WERE A FEW SNAPPED PINE TREES AS WELL. PATH LENGTH 1/2 MILE. MAX PATH WIDTH 200 YARDS.
THE PUBLIC REPORTED A FALLEN TREE WHICH HAS DAMAGED A MOBILE HOME IN CHARLESTON. THIS REPORT WAS RECEIVED VIA LOCAL TELEVISION METEOROLOGIST.

A METAL GARAGE WAS EXTENSIVELY DAMAGED AT THE INTERSECTION OF TATUM POND ROAD AND HIGHWAY 32. A COUPLE OF MOBILE HOMES EXPERIENCED ROOF DAMAGE FURTHER DOWN TATUM POND ROAD. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. PATH LENGTH 2 MILES. MAX PATH WIDTH 100 YARDS.

A BROADCAST METEOROLOGIST WITH WABG-TV REPORTS THAT A CAR WAS STRUCK BY A TREE IN TILLATOBA. IN ADDITION...NUMEROUS BUILDINGS HAVE SUSTAINED MAJOR DAMAGE AND LIGHT POLES HAVE BEEN SNAPPED IN HALF. NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES HAVE BEEN REPORTED AT THIS TIME.

SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS FIRST OBSERVED AT AN AUTOMOTIVE REPAIR SHOP WHICH SUFFERED MAJOR METAL ROOF DAMAGE AND WALL FAILURE ON THE REAR OF THE BUILDING. DAMAGE CONTINUED FURTHER TO THE SOUTHEAST WHERE A HOME LOST A MAJORITY OF ITS ROOF. FURTHER SOUTHEAST...ROOF DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED TO A FEW MOBILE HOMES AND OUTBUILDINGS...WITH ONE SMALL MOBILE HOME ROLLED ON ITS SIDE. PATH LENGTH 1 MILE. MAX WIDTH 1/3 MILE.
